english.technical: GLM analysis revealed {num_clusters} significant clusters (p < {threshold}, {correction} correction). Peak activation in {peak_region} at MNI coordinates [{peak_x}, {peak_y}, {peak_z}] with Z = {z_score}. Cluster extent: {cluster_size} voxels.

structured.summary: Analysis identified {num_clusters} brain regions with significant activation during {task}.
structured.methodology: We used {method} with {correction} correction for multiple comparisons at {threshold} significance level.
structured.findings: Peak activation was observed in {peak_region} (Z = {z_score}, {cluster_size} voxels).
structured.implications: This activation pattern is consistent with {cognitive_function} and suggests {interpretation}.

english.layman: We found {num_clusters} brain regions that were particularly active during the {task} task. The strongest response was in the {peak_region}, which is known to be involved in {function_description}.

spanish.technical: El análisis GLM reveló {num_clusters} grupos significativos (p < {threshold}, corrección {correction}). Activación máxima en {peak_region} en coordenadas MNI [{peak_x}, {peak_y}, {peak_z}] con Z = {z_score}. Extensión del grupo: {cluster_size} vóxeles.

structured.summary: El análisis identificó {num_clusters} regiones cerebrales con activación significativa durante {task}.
structured.methodology: Utilizamos {method} con corrección {correction} para comparaciones múltiples al nivel de significancia {threshold}.
structured.findings: La activación máxima se observó en {peak_region} (Z = {z_score}, {cluster_size} vóxeles).
structured.implications: Este patrón de activación es consistente con {cognitive_function} y sugiere {interpretation}.

spanish.layman: Encontramos {num_clusters} regiones cerebrales que estuvieron particularmente activas durante la tarea {task}. La respuesta más fuerte fue en {peak_region}, que se sabe está involucrada en {function_description}.

french.technical: L'analyse GLM a révélé {num_clusters} clusters significatifs (p < {threshold}, correction {correction}). Activation maximale dans {peak_region} aux coordonnées MNI [{peak_x}, {peak_y}, {peak_z}] avec Z = {z_score}. Étendue du cluster: {cluster_size} voxels.
french.layman: Nous avons trouvé {num_clusters} régions cérébrales particulièrement actives pendant la tâche {task}. La réponse la plus forte était dans {peak_region}, qui est connue pour être impliquée dans {function_description}.

english.technical: Functional connectivity analysis using {method} revealed significant connections between {seed_region} and {target_regions}. Network properties: modularity = {modularity}, clustering coefficient = {clustering}, characteristic path length = {path_length}.

structured.summary: Connectivity analysis identified significant communication patterns between brain regions.
structured.methodology: We computed {method} connectivity using {seed_region} as seed region.
structured.implications: This connectivity pattern suggests coordinated activity within the {network_name} network.

english.layman: We examined how different brain regions communicate with each other. We found that {seed_region} has strong connections with {target_regions}, indicating they work together during {task}.

spanish.technical: El análisis de conectividad funcional usando {method} reveló conexiones significativas entre {seed_region} y {target_regions}. Propiedades de la red: modularidad = {modularity}, coeficiente de agrupamiento = {clustering}, longitud característica del camino = {path_length}.
spanish.layman: Examinamos cómo se comunican entre sí las diferentes regiones del cerebro. Encontramos que {seed_region} tiene conexiones fuertes con {target_regions}, indicando que trabajan juntas durante {task}.

english.technical: Statistical parametric mapping using {model} revealed significant effects (F({df1},{df2}) = {f_statistic}, p = {p_value}). Post-hoc tests showed {significant_contrasts} with effect sizes ranging from {min_effect_size} to {max_effect_size} (Cohen's d).

structured.summary: Statistical analysis detected significant effects in the brain activation data.
structured.methodology: We applied {model} statistical model with {contrast_description} contrasts.
structured.findings: Significant effects were found with F = {f_statistic} and p = {p_value}.
structured.implications: The effect sizes suggest {effect_interpretation} differences between conditions.

english.layman: Our statistical analysis found significant differences in brain activity between different conditions. These differences are {effect_interpretation} and suggest that the brain responds differently to {condition_description}.

english.technical: Processing failed during {stage} with error: {error_type}. Stack trace: {stack_trace}. Suggested resolution: {resolution}.

structured.summary: An error occurred during data processing that prevented completion.

english.layman: We encountered a problem while processing your data during the {stage} step. {user_friendly_explanation}. Here's what you can try: {simple_resolution}.

spanish.technical: El procesamiento falló durante {stage} con error: {error_type}. Seguimiento de pila: {stack_trace}. Resolución sugerida: {resolution}.
spanish.layman: Encontramos un problema mientras procesábamos sus datos durante el paso {stage}. {user_friendly_explanation}. Esto es lo que puede intentar: {simple_resolution}.

english.technical: Data quality assessment identified {num_issues} issues: {issue_list}. Quality metrics: mean FD = {mean_fd}, DVARS = {dvars}, tSNR = {tsnr}. Recommendation: {qc_recommendation}.

structured.summary: Quality control analysis detected issues that may affect results reliability.
structured.issues: The following data quality issues were identified: {detailed_issues}

english.layman: We found some quality issues with your brain imaging data. This means the results might not be as reliable as we'd like. The main issues were: {simple_issue_list}. We suggest: {simple_recommendation}.

We're currently working on step {current_stage} of {total_stages}: {stage_description}. We've finished {completed_subjects} out of {total_subjects} participants ({progress_percentage}% complete). Estimated time remaining: {estimated_time_remaining}.

spanish.technical: Etapa de preprocesamiento {current_stage}/{total_stages}: {stage_name}. Progreso: {completed_subjects}/{total_subjects} sujetos ({progress_percentage}%). ETA: {estimated_time_remaining}.
spanish.layman: Actualmente estamos trabajando en el paso {current_stage} de {total_stages}: {stage_description}. Hemos terminado {completed_subjects} de {total_subjects} participantes ({progress_percentage}% completo). Tiempo estimado restante: {estimated_time_remaining}.

english.technical: Analysis pipeline progress: {completed_steps}/{total_steps} steps completed. Current: {current_analysis} ({current_progress}%). Queue status: {queued_jobs} jobs pending.
english.layman: Analysis is {current_progress}% complete. We're currently running {current_analysis}. There are {queued_jobs} more analyses in the queue after this one.

english.technical: Based on current results, recommend: 1) {technical_recommendation_1}, 2) {technical_recommendation_2}, 3) {technical_recommendation_3}. Consider additional analyses: {suggested_analyses}.
english.layman: Based on what we found, here's what we suggest doing next: {simple_recommendations}. You might also want to consider: {additional_suggestions}.

spanish.technical: Basado en los resultados actuales, recomendamos: 1) {technical_recommendation_1}, 2) {technical_recommendation_2}, 3) {technical_recommendation_3}. Considerar análisis adicionales: {suggested_analyses}.
spanish.layman: Basado en lo que encontramos, esto es lo que sugerimos hacer después: {simple_recommendations}. También podría considerar: {additional_suggestions}.

english.technical: For your research question, consider: {method_1} (advantages: {advantages_1}), {method_2} (advantages: {advantages_2}). Sample size recommendations: {sample_size_guidance}.
english.layman: For what you're trying to study, we suggest using {simple_method_description}. This approach is good because {method_benefits}.

english.technical: {concept} refers to {technical_definition}. Key characteristics: {characteristics}. Mathematical formulation: {formula}. Applications: {applications}.

structured.relevance: This is relevant to neuroimaging because: {neuroimaging_relevance}

english.layman: {concept} is {simple_definition}. Think of it like {analogy}. In brain imaging, we use this to {practical_application}.

spanish.technical: {concept} se refiere a {technical_definition}. Características clave: {characteristics}. Formulación matemática: {formula}. Aplicaciones: {applications}.
spanish.layman: {concept} es {simple_definition}. Piénsalo como {analogy}. En imágenes cerebrales, usamos esto para {practical_application}.

english.technical: Issue: {problem_description}. Diagnostic steps: {diagnostic_steps}. Common causes: {common_causes}. Resolution approaches: {resolution_approaches}.

structured.prevention: To prevent this in the future: {prevention_tips}

english.layman: The problem seems to be: {simple_problem_description}. Here are some things you can try to fix it: {simple_solutions}.

p_value.english: Think of a p-value like a probability of getting a false alarm. Just as a smoke detector might go off when you're cooking (false alarm), a small p-value means it's unlikely our result is just a 'false alarm' due to chance.
p_value.spanish: Piensa en un valor p como la probabilidad de obtener una falsa alarma. Así como un detector de humo podría sonar cuando estás cocinando (falsa alarma), un valor p pequeño significa que es improbable que nuestro resultado sea solo una 'falsa alarma' debido al azar.

multiple_comparisons.english: Multiple comparisons are like buying many lottery tickets. The more tickets you buy, the higher your chance of winning something, but most will still be losers. Similarly, testing many brain regions increases the chance of finding false positives.

effect_size.english: Effect size is like the volume of music. Statistical significance tells you the music is definitely playing (not silence), but effect size tells you if it's a whisper or a concert.

bold_signal.english: The BOLD signal is like the glow of a coal in a fire. When a brain region works hard, it needs more oxygen (like a coal getting brighter when you blow on it), and that's what we measure.

preprocessing.english: Preprocessing brain images is like editing a photo. Just as you might crop, adjust brightness, and remove red-eye to get a clear picture, we clean up brain images to see the actual brain activity clearly.

normalization.english: Brain normalization is like fitting everyone's head into the same hat size for comparison. Since everyone's brain is shaped slightly differently, we 'stretch' or 'squeeze' them to fit a standard template.

glm.english: A General Linear Model is like a recipe that explains what ingredients (brain activity) contribute to the final dish (behavior). We can see which ingredients have the biggest impact on the taste.

connectivity.english: Brain connectivity is like a telephone network. We can see which brain regions 'call' each other frequently and which ones rarely communicate, helping us understand how information flows.

fMRI.technical: Functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging - a neuroimaging technique that measures brain activity by detecting changes in blood oxygenation and flow
fMRI.simple: a brain scanner that shows which parts of the brain are active
fMRI.explanation: uses magnetic fields to detect blood flow changes in the brain

BOLD.technical: Blood Oxygen Level Dependent signal - the physiological basis of fMRI contrast
BOLD.simple: the signal we measure that shows brain activity
BOLD.explanation: when brain cells work harder, they need more oxygen, which changes the magnetic properties we can detect

GLM.technical: General Linear Model - a statistical framework for analyzing neuroimaging data
GLM.simple: a mathematical method to find patterns in brain activity
GLM.explanation: helps us determine which brain regions are significantly active during different tasks

preprocessing.technical: the initial processing steps applied to raw neuroimaging data to prepare it for statistical analysis
preprocessing.simple: cleaning and preparing the brain images for analysis
preprocessing.explanation: removes artifacts and standardizes the data so we can compare across subjects

p_value.technical: the probability of obtaining results at least as extreme as observed, assuming the null hypothesis is true
p_value.simple: a number that tells us how likely our results are due to chance
p_value.explanation: smaller p-values (like 0.01) mean it's very unlikely the result is just random chance

FWE.technical: Family-Wise Error rate - a method for controlling Type I error when making multiple statistical comparisons
FWE.simple: a way to avoid false positives when testing many brain regions
FWE.explanation: ensures that even when testing thousands of brain regions, we maintain reliable results

cluster.technical: a contiguous group of voxels that exceed a statistical threshold
cluster.simple: a group of nearby brain locations that show similar activity
cluster.explanation: rather than looking at individual points, we look at connected regions of activity
